metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting opportunities for your 3-year-old! Get ready for a day of creativity at the Horniman Museum with their Women's History Month crafts, or enjoy the lively Magic of India Holi Colour Dance Festival. For those looking to explore, the Southbank Centre offers installations and open spaces perfect for a family outing.

activities.title: The Magic of India Holi Colour Dance Festival
activities.description: Lively dance performances including bangra and dhol troops, DJs, a dancing colour-play area with safe (washable) powder colours, and Indian food stalls
activities.whyItWorks: The colours, music, and movement will captivate a 3-year-old. The open space allows for running around.

transport.tip: Bring a change of clothes!

activities.title: Family Craft Saturdays: Women's History Month
activities.description: Celebrate Women's History Month and create a mini botanical painting inspired by pioneer Anna Atkins, an artist and botanist
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on craft activity suitable for little ones, inspired by nature and history. The Horniman also has gardens to explore.

transport.tip: Check out the museum's gardens after the craft session.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small, friendly city farm with various animals. Perfect for younger kids.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love animals, and this farm is easily accessible and free.

transport.tip: Check for feeding times.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Formerly the Museum of Childhood, this recently renovated museum offers hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions designed to spark creativity and imagination in children.
activities.whyItWorks: The interactive exhibits and playful atmosphere are perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Tube to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check the website for special events.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with a community feel near Brick Lane. They often have special Easter activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love animals, and the farm provides a nice outdoor experience in the city.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Shoreditch High Street or Liverpool Street
transport.tip: Check their website for Easter activity schedules.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: The redeveloped area of the 2012 Olympics offers vast outdoor spaces, playgrounds and walking trails.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space for running around, and interesting sights for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, depending on location within the park. Central Line to Stratford is a good starting point.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events and specific playground locations.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre: Tiny Planet
activities.description: A dynamic performance for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Although slightly above the age range, it can still be a fun introduction to theater

transport.tip: Check showtimes in advance

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers, this playground offers a safe and engaging environment.

transport.tip: Located near Westfield Stratford City for easy access to amenities.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, volcanoes and mammals.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Interactive exhibits and impressive displays will captivate a 3-year-old.

transport.tip: Visit the Dinosaurs gallery first, it's the most popular.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits including space exploration and transport.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and space exploration will fascinate young minds.

transport.tip: Visit the Pattern Pod for under 5's

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Explore art and design from around the world. The museum offers daily free activities and events for children.
activities.whyItWorks: The V&A offers a wide range of art and design exhibits to explore with a 3 year old.

transport.tip: Borrow a backpack of activities for kids aged 5-12.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: The views and indoor garden provide a unique experience for children.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance. Tickets are released on Mondays, about three weeks in advance.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.address: Pier Street, Isle of Dogs, London E14 3HP
activities.description: One of the largest city farms in London, with free entry and plenty of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of open space for running around and a wide variety of animals to see.

transport.tip: Check for special events and activities.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Offers scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo (entry fee applies for the zoo).
activities.whyItWorks: Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

transport.tip: Bring a picnic blanket for a relaxing lunch by the lake.

activities.title: Hyde Park
activities.description: Take a stroll along the Serpentine, and toddlers will love to look at the ducks! Diana Memorial Playground
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run and play

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Lancaster Gate, Marble Arch, Hyde Park Corner
transport.tip: Afterwards, why not head over to Kensington gardens to explore the Diana Memorial Playground, complete with a pirate ship and sensory trails.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: This pirate ship wooden playground is so fun for children to explore!
activities.whyItWorks: Kids will love running around a pirate ship

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Lancaster Gate, Marble Arch, Hyde Park Corner
transport.tip: There are excellent baby and child facilities

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt
activities.description: Join the Marvellous Mouse Twins for a Whisker-Twitching Easter Adventure! Step into a magical woodland world filled with dress-up, storytelling, songs, games, and a thrilling quest to find the hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: The interactive, character-led adventure will capture a child's imagination. The outdoor setting allows for movement and exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Edgware Road or Paddington Station
transport.tip: Arrive early as spaces are limited for each session.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that motivates kids to walk and explore. Ends with chocolate!

transport.tip: Go early in the day as the grounds are calmer.

activities.title: Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a beloved children's show with outdoor exploration and learning about nature.

transport.fromHackney: Tube or train to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Allow 2-3 hours to do the trail properly.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize! In addition there is arts and crafts activities and Easter Bunny crowns.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with an Easter egg hunt, appealing to kids' love of animals and treasure hunts.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town
transport.tip: Pick up a trail card to participate in the hunt.

activities.title: Spring Fair
activities.description: Join in the seasonal fun and games at the Horniman Spring Fair, with live music, crafty workshops, tours and lots more on the schedule
activities.whyItWorks: Live music, crafty workshops, tours and lots more on the schedule.

transport.tip: Book in advance

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: This immersive family exhibition invites children to step into the magical world of classic fairy tales through beautiful illustrations, pop-up books, original manuscripts and even puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive elements throughout – including a magic mirror, a genie and even magical potions – making it more than just a display to look at.

transport.fromHackney: Tube or Train to Euston Road
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Step aboard Cutty Sark in Greenwich over the long weekend, and take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks — and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: A great activity for slightly older kids.

transport.tip: Arrive early.

activities.title: Gozzle Nature Trail
activities.address: Queen Elizabeth Walk, Barnes, London SW13 9WT
activities.description: Follow an interactive outdoor trail charting Gozzle's journey from her first nibble to her first splash – and eventually her first migration.
activities.whyItWorks: Along the way, children will hop across stepping stones, practise funny animal poses, use their sense of smell to “forage” for food and keep watch for 15 mini Gozzles hidden around the route.

transport.tip: Arrive early.
